text.title: Basic Python Type Hints
text.summary: Variable annotations, function signatures, X | None unions, and modern collection syntax for Python 3.9+.

rule.statement: Use X | None instead of Optional[X] and list[T] instead of typing.List[T] for all new Python 3.9+ code. Enforce via ruff rule UP007.
rule.rationale: Modern syntax is more readable. Mixing Optional[X] with X | None in one codebase triggers ruff warnings and degrades readability. Pick one and enforce it project-wide.

rule.statement: Always annotate public function parameters and return types. Internal helpers: annotate args + return; annotate locals only if mypy complains.
rule.rationale: Public interfaces are the primary value of type hints. Over-annotating every local variable adds noise without proportional benefit.

example.code:
# Wrong: old syntax, Optional import, no return type from typing import Optional, List
def get_user(user_id: int) -> Optional["User"]: # use User | None ...
def get_names() -> List[str]: # use list[str] ...

from typing import TypeAlias
UserId: TypeAlias = int Handler: TypeAlias = Callable[["Request"], "Response"]
